tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and mild hyperchloremia with low CO₂, indicating possible metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and abnormal urine appearance, supporting renal dysfunction and possible dehydration or catabolic state. Low HbA1c with normoglycemia may reflect recent changes or anemia. Recommend further evaluation for acute or chronic kidney disease and assessment for underlying causes such as diabetes, infection, or dehydration.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
